value_post.text:
The difference between people who hit their goals and people who don't?

A system.

Not motivation. Not willpower. A repeatable daily system that connects small habits to big outcomes.

Here's how to build one:

1. Set 2-3 objectives (not 10)
2. Define measurable key results for each
3. Link daily habits to those key results
4. Track streaks — momentum is everything
5. Review weekly. Adjust monthly.

Consistency > intensity. Every single time.

thread_post.text:
I tracked my habits for 365 days straight.

Here are 5 things I learned about building habits that actually stick:

🧵 (thread)

1/ Start embarrassingly small.

Don't commit to "read 1 hour daily." Commit to "read 1 page."

The goal isn't the activity — it's showing up. Once the streak starts, your brain protects it.

2/ Connect habits to goals.

Random habits feel pointless after a week. But when "meditate 10 min" is linked to your goal of "reduce stress by Q2" — every check-in has meaning.

This is why OKRs work for personal life, not just companies.

3/ Review weekly. Not daily.

Daily reflection leads to overthinking. Weekly reviews give you enough data to spot patterns without burning out on self-analysis.

Every Sunday: What worked? What didn't? What changes next week?

5/ Use a system, not an app.

Apps send notifications you ignore. A well-designed system in Notion becomes your command center.

I built exactly this: Habit Tracker & Goal System.
OKR goals + streak tracking + reviews + rewards.
